Title: A sucrose non-fermenting-1-related protein kinase-1 gene, IbSnRK1, improves starch content, composition, granule size, degree of crystallinity and gelatinization in transgenic sweet potato

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major carbohydrate reserve; important for food and industrial starch applications
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased starch content, decreased amylose proportion, enlarged starch granule size, increased crystallinity, and lowered gelatinization temperature/enthalpy in storage roots.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive regulation of starch biosynthesis gene expression and enzyme activities
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: starch biosynthesis; carbon partitioning into starch; starch composition remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: VacINV; NI; SuSy; HXK; UGPase; PGM; AGP-L1; GBSSI; SSI; SSII; SSIII; SBEII; PUL
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates VacINV, NI, SuSy, HXK, UGPase, PGM, AGP-L1, GBSSI, SSI, SSII, SSIII and SBEII; downregulates PUL; no obvious change in GBSSII, SSIV, SBEI and IsaI.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as a major positive controller of starch accumulation and starch quality traits in sweet potato storage roots.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in storage roots; strongly induced by 175 mM sucrose in leaves; circadian rhythm with peak at 10 am.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in storage roots; overexpressed in transgenic storage roots under CaMV 35S promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in sweet potato; qRT-PCR; metabolite quantification; starch granule morphology/size analysis; X-ray diffraction; DSC thermal analysis; enzyme activity assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 175 mM sucrose treatment; circadian light/dark regimen; greenhouse and field growth
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological variations observed in transgenic plants under tested conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for improving starch yield and quality in sweet potato and potentially other crops; useful for engineering low-amylose, larger-granule starch with favorable industrial properties.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes this as the first report showing SnRK1 improves starch quality traits, not just starch amount, in plants.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IbSnRK1 expression positively correlated with starch content among sweet potato cultivars and was highest in storage roots. 2. IbSnRK1 was induced by sucrose and showed circadian rhythmic expression. 3. Overexpression in cv. Lizixiang increased starch content and reduced amylose proportion in storage roots. 4. Transgenic lines had more and larger starch granules, altered amylopectin chain-length distribution, higher crystallinity, and lower gelatinization temperature/enthalpy. 5. IbSnRK1 overexpression systemically upregulated many starch biosynthetic genes, increased activities of key enzymes, and elevated ADP-glucose, supporting enhanced flux into starch biosynthesis.
